steps.title: Apply the margin of error to find the plausible range
steps.content:
The sample estimate is 15% with a margin of error of 2%. The plausible range for the true percentage is:
$$15\% - 2\% = 13\% \quad \text{to} \quad 15\% + 2\% = 17\%$$

steps.title: Convert percentage bounds to actual bead counts
steps.content:
With 10,000 total beads:
Lower bound: $13\% \times 10{,}000 = 1{,}300$
Upper bound: $17\% \times 10{,}000 = 1{,}700$
So the plausible range is $1{,}300 \lt r \lt 1{,}700$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• "r is greater than 1,700": 1,700 is the upper bound of the plausible range; values above it are outside the margin of error. ✗
  • "1,300 is less than r, which is less than 1,700": This is exactly the interval 13%–17% of 10,000, matching the estimate ± margin. ✓
  • "200 is less than r, which is less than 1,500": 1,500 corresponds to 15%, the point estimate; values between some lower limit and 1,500 cover only part of the plausible range and exclude the upper half. ✗
  • "r is less than 1,300": 1,300 is the lower bound; values below it are outside the margin of error. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
